title: Battle of Raszyn
description: War of the Fifth Coalition. armies of the Austrian Empire under Archduke Ferdinand Karl Joseph of Austria-Este defeated the Duchy of Warsaw under Józef Antoni Poniatowski

title: Napoleon officially marries Marie Louise, Duchess of Parma.
description: This marriage was intended to secure an alliance between France and Austria and produce an heir for Napoleon.

title: Napoleon's abdication
description: In the night of 5-6 April, after long attempts at negotiations with the allies, Napoleon accepted defeat and wrote out a short text declaring that he had abdicated

title: Joseph Bonaparte becomes King of Naples
description: Joseph Bonaparte, Napoleon's older brother, is crowned King of Naples.

title: Batalla de Abensberg
description: The Battle of Abensberg was a battle of the War of the Fifth Coalition in which the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte defeated the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen.

title: Battle of Landshut
description: The Battle of Landshut was a battle of the War of the Fifth Coalition in which the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte defeated the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen.

title: Battle of Eggmühl
description: The Battle of Eggmühl was a battle of the War of the Fifth Coalition in which the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte defeated the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen.

title: Battle of Teugn-Hausen
description: The Battle of Teugn-Hausen was a battle of the War of the Fifth Coalition in which the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte defeated the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen.

title: Battle of Thann
description: The Battle of Thann was a battle of the War of the Fifth Coalition in which the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte defeated the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen.

title: Battle of Pfaffenhausen
description: The Battle of Pfaffenhausen was a battle of the War of the Fifth Coalition in which the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte defeated the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen.

title: Battle of Günzburg
description: The Battle of Günzburg was a battle of the War of the Fifth Coalition in which the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte defeated the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen.

title: Battle of La Rothière
description: The Battle of La Rothière was a battle of the War of the Sixth Coalition in which the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte defeated the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen.

title: Battle of Corunna
description: The Battle of Corunna was a battle of the Peninsular War in which the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte defeated the British army under Arthur Wellesley.

title: Battle of Copenhagen
description: The Battle of Copenhagen was a naval battle of the Napoleonic Wars in which the British Royal Navy under the command of Admiral Sir Hyde Parker, with Horatio Nelson commanding the van, defeated the Danish-Norwegian fleet.

title: Battle of Sabugal
description: The Battle of Sabugal was a battle of the Peninsular War in which the British army under Arthur Wellesley defeated the French army under Marshal Masséna.

title: Battle of Fuentes de Oñoro
description: The Battle of Fuentes de Oñoro was a battle of the Peninsular War in which the British army under Arthur Wellesley defeated the French army under Marshal Masséna.

title: Siege of Almeida
description: The Siege of Almeida was a siege of the Peninsular War in which the British army under Arthur Wellesley defeated the French army under Marshal Masséna.

title: Battle of Almeida
description: The Battle of Fuentes de Oñoro was a battle of the Peninsular War in which the British army under Arthur Wellesley defeated the French army under Marshal Masséna.

title: Siege of Ferrara
description: The Siege of Ferrara was part of the Neapolitan War in which the Austrian army under the command of Lieutenant Field Marshal Friedrich Bianchi defeated the Neapolitan army under the command of Joachim Murat.

title: Battle of Tolentino
description: The Battle of Tolentino was part of the Neapolitan War in which the Austrian army under the command of Lieutenant Field Marshal Friedrich Bianchi defeated the Neapolitan army under the command of Joachim Murat.

title: Battle of Magnano
description: The Battle of Magnano was part of the War of the Second Coalition in which the Austrian army under the command of Lieutenant Field Marshal Friedrich Bianchi defeated the Neapolitan army under the command of Joachim Murat.

title: Battle of Trebbia
description: The Battle of Trebbia was part of the War of the Second Coalition in which the Austrian army under the command of Lieutenant Field Marshal Friedrich Bianchi defeated the Neapolitan army under the command of Joachim Murat.

title: Storming of Badajoz
description: The Storming of Badajoz was part of the Peninsular War in which the British army under Arthur Wellesley defeated the French army under Marshal Masséna.

title: Siege of Ciudad Rodrigo
description: The siege of Ciudad Rodrigo was the successful investment of the French-occupied city of Ciudad Rodrigo by Lord Wellington's Anglo-Portuguese Army from 7–20 January 1812.

title: Beginning of the Italian Campaign
description: Napoleon begins his offensive against the Austrian army.

title: Battle of Marengo
description: The Battle of Marengo was a battle of the War of the Second Coalition in which First Consul Napoleon Bonaparte and Austrian forces near the city of Alessandria, in Piedmont, Italy. Near the end of the day, the French overcame General Michael von Melas's surprise attack, drove the Austrians out of Italy

title: Battle of Montenotte
description: Napoleon's first victory in his first Italian campaign.

title: Battle of Toulouse
description: Wellington defeats Soult in France, unaware that Napoleon has already abdicated.

title: Battle of Millesimo
description: The Battle of Millesimo, fought on 13 and 14 April 1796, was the name that Napoleon Bonaparte gave in his correspondence to one of a series of small battles that were fought in Liguria, Northern Italy.

title: Second Battle of Dego
description: The Second Battle of Dego was fought on 14 and 15 April 1796 during the French Revolutionary Wars between French forces and Austro-Sardinian forces. The battle was fought near Dego, a hamlet in northwestern Italy, and ended in a French victory.

title: Battle of Mount Tabor
description: Battle between French forces commanded by Napoleon Bonaparte and General Jean-Baptiste Kléber, against an Ottoman Army under Abdullah Pasha al-Azm, ruler of Damascus.

title: Siege of Genoa begins
description: The siege of Genoa saw an Austrian army led by Michael von Melas besiege the port of Genoa, which was occupied by a French garrison under the command of André Massena.

title: Storming of Ratisbon
description: Also called the Battle of Regensburg, was fought between the army of the French Empire, led by Napoleon I, and that of the Austrian Empire, led by Archduke Charles.

title: Battle of Neumarkt-Sankt Veit
description: Austrian victory over Marshal Bessières.

title: Armistice of Cherasco
description: Signed between Victor Amadeus III of Sardinia and Napoleon Bonaparte. It withdrew Sardinia from the War of the First Coalition

title: End of the Peninsular War
description: Official conclusion of fighting in the Peninsula.

title: Battle of Cassano
description: The clash is part of the battle of the Adda River or the so-called forcing of the Adda, which on the first day resulted in a minor victory for the Russians under the Count Suvorov over Barthélemy Schérer's French forces at Lecco.

title: Battle of Landshut
description: Napoleon's capture of a major Austrian supply center.

title: Battle of Alcolea
description: This was the first pitched battle in Andalusia between a more or less organized Spanish army and French forces.

title: Acción de la Fuensanta
description: La acción de la Fuensanta fue un combate llevado a cabo el 12 de noviembre de 1810 entre las tropas de Pedro Villacampa y las del general Józef Chlopicki en el Santuario de la Fuensanta de Villel.

title: Acción de Orgaz
description: Enfrentó a una columna del Tercer Ejército español compuesta por unos ciento veinte jinetes y doscientos infantes, al mando del coronel Marcelino Villa, con una fuerza francesa de unos ochocientos jinetes pertenecientes a unidades de húsares y cazadores a caballo.

title: Batalla de Alcañiz
description: Tuvo lugar en la localidad turolense de Alcañiz el 23 de mayo de 1809 y donde las tropas españolas al mando de Joaquín Blake vencieron a las francesas comandadas por Louis Gabriel Suchet..

title: Batalla de Usagre
description: La batalla de Usagre se desarrolló el 25 de mayo de 1811 en Usagre, España, y enfrentó la caballería francesa del general de Latour-Maubourg a la caballería anglo-hispano-portuguesa del mayor general William Lumley. El enfrentamiento terminó con una victoria de los aliados hispano-anglo-portugueses.

title: Batalla del Bidasoa
description: La batalla de Bidasoa, también conocida como batalla de Larrún, fue una batalla librada el 7 de octubre de 1813 entre el ejército francés comandado por el mariscal Soult y el ejército aliado anglo-hispano-portugués comandado por el duque de Wellington. La batalla terminó con una victoria aliada.

title: Batalla del Puente de Triana
description: La batalla del Puente de Triana tuvo lugar el 27 de agosto de 1812 para poder tomar la ciudad de Sevilla.

title: Batalla de Cabezón
description: El 12 de junio de 1808 se produjo el primer intento del general García de la Cuesta de cortar las comunicaciones entre Madrid e Irún, cuando se produjeron sublevaciones en España tras el levantamiento del 2 de mayo.

title: Sitio de Cádiz
description: El asedio de Cádiz es como se le conoce al sitio de Cádiz y San Fernando que tuvo lugar el 5 de febrero de 1810, estableciendo un cerco a la ciudad de Cádiz y San Fernando hasta el 24 de agosto de 1812.

title: Batalla de Arroyomolinos
description: On 28 October 1811 an allied force under General Rowland Hill trapped and defeated a French force under General Jean-Baptiste Girard.

title: Batalla de Medina de Rioseco
description: El 14 de julio de 1808 en las inmediaciones de Medina de Rioseco (actualmente en la provincia de Valladolid) se enfrentaron el Ejército español comandado conjuntamente por los generales españoles García de la Cuesta y Joaquín Blake frente al mariscal Jean-Baptiste Bessières que término con el triunfo francés.

title: Toma del Fuerte del Rastrillar de Laredo
description: Fue una rápida operación, lograda en apenas dos días debido a la baja moral de los defensores italianos, aliados de Francia. Tras amenazar con no dar cuartel, las tropas españolas forzaron la rendición cuando los soldados italianos apresaron a sus oficiales franceses, facilitando la conquista con bajas mínimas.

title: Batalla de Fuentes de Oñoro
description: Entre el 3 y el 5 de mayo de 1811 se enfrentó el ejército Británico-Portugués con el ejército francés para disminuir la presión del sitio de la ciudad de Almeida.

title: Batalla de Roliça
description: 17 August 1808, was the first battle fought by the British army during the Peninsular War.

title: Batalla de Vimieiro
description: On 21 August 1808, the British under General Arthur Wellesley (who later became the Duke of Wellington) defeated the French under Major-General Jean-Andoche Junot near the village of Vimeiro.

title: Segona Batalla de Porto
description: El 12 de maig de 1809 va ser una contesa que va acabar amb la victòria per a l'exèrcit anglo-portuguès comandat per Sir Arthur Wellesley sobre l'exèrcit francès del mariscal Nicolas Jean de Dieu Soult.

title: Batalla de García Hernández
description: Fue una acción militar librada el 23 de julio de 1812 entre la caballería ligera del ejército británico y la caballería francesa.

title: Batalla de Majadahonda
description: The confrontation took place on August 11, 1812, where Napoleonic troops led by General Anne-François-Charles Trelliard defeated Anglo-Portuguese forces led by George Bock and Benjamin d’Urban.

title: Batalla de Caldiero
description: Fought on November 12th 1796 it was the first defeat Bonaparte suffered in open field. But his victory at Arcole just five days later help to minimize this defeat.

title: Coronation of Emperor Alexander I
description: The coronation of Emperor Alexander I and Elizabeth Alexeyevna took place on 15 September 1801 in the Dormition Cathedral of the Moscow Kremlin.

title: Battle of Aspern-Essling
description: The Battle of Aspern-Essling was a major engagement of the Napoleonic Wars, fought on May 21–22, 1809, between the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte and the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen. It marked Napoleon's first personal defeat in a major battle, as the Austrians successfully defended their ground and forced the French to retreat across the Danube River.

title: Battle of Wagram
description: The Battle of Wagram was a major engagement of the Napoleonic Wars, fought on July 5–6, 1809, between the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte and the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen.

title: Battle of Leipzig
description: Known as the Battle of the Nations, was the largest battle in the Napoleonic Wars. Napoleon's defeat by a coalition of Austrian, Prussian, Russian, and Swedish forces led to his first exile.

title: Battle of Borodino
description: It was the bloodiest single-day battle in the Napoleonic Wars. Though technically a French victory, the massive losses weakened Napoleon's Grande Armée and foreshadowed the disastrous retreat from Moscow.

title: Battle of Corunna / Elviña
description: The Battle of Corunna (or A Coruña, La Corunna, La Coruña or La Corogne), in Spain known as Battle of Elviña, took place on 16 January 1809, when a French corps under Marshal of the Empire Jean de Dieu Soult attacked a British army under Lieutenant-General Sir John Moore. The battle took place during the Peninsular War, which was part of the wider Napoleonic Wars.

title: Battle of the Pyramids
description: It was a decisive French victory during Napoleon's Egyptian campaign. Napoleon famously addressed his troops: 'Soldiers, from the height of these pyramids, forty centuries look down upon you.'

title: Battle of Waterloo
description: The Battle of Waterloo on June 18, 1815 was Napoleon's final defeat. The Duke of Wellington and Prussian Marshal Blücher's combined forces ended Napoleon's rule as Emperor of France. The Lion's Mound memorial overlooks the battlefield.

title: Battle of Jena
description: Napoleon crushed the Prussian army at the Battle of Jena-Auerstedt. This decisive victory led to the French occupation of most of Prussia and cemented Napoleon's control over Central Europe.

title: Battle of Arcole
description: The Battle of Arcole or Battle of Arcola (15–17 November 1796) was fought between French and Austrian forces 25 kilometres (16 mi) southeast of Verona during the War of the First Coalition, a part of the French Revolutionary Wars.

title: Battle of Arcis-sur-Aube
description: The Battle of Arcis-sur-Aube (20-21 March 1814) saw an Imperial French army under Napoleon face a much larger Allied army led by Karl Philipp, Prince of Schwarzenberg during the War of the Sixth Coalition.

title: Battle of Auerstaedt
description: This battle pitted the first Prussian army under King Frederick William III of Prussia against part of the French army led by Louis Nicolas Davout on October 14, 1806, parallel to the Battle of Jena, which was fought on the same date.

title: Battle of Bautzen
description: The Battle of Bautzen (20-21 May 1813) saw an Imperial French army under Napoleon face a much larger Allied army led by Karl Philipp, Prince of Schwarzenberg during the War of the Sixth Coalition.

title: Battle of Wagram
description: The Battle of Wagram was a major engagement of the Napoleonic Wars, fought on July 5–6, 1809, between the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte and the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen.

title: Battle of Ulm
description: The Battle of Ulm was a major engagement of the Napoleonic Wars, fought on October 16–19, 1805, between the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte and the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen.

title: Battle of Trafalgar
description: The Battle of Trafalgar was fought on October 21, 1805, between the British Royal Navy and the combined fleets of the French and Spanish Navies.

title: Battle of Eylau
description: The Battle of Eylau (also known as the Battle of Preussisch-Eylau) was a bloody and strategically inconclusive battle on 7 and 8 February [O.S. 26 and 27 Jan.] 1807 between Napoleon's Grande Armée and the Imperial Russian Army under the command of General Levin August von Bennigsen

title: Battle of Friedland
description: Fought on June 14, 1807, between the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte and the Russian army under General Levin August von Bennigsen

title: Battle of Berezina
description: Battle between Napoleon's Grande Armée and the Imperial Russian Army under Field Marshal Wittgenstein and Admiral Chichagov. Napoleon was retreating toward Poland in chaos after the aborted occupation of Moscow and trying to cross the Berezina River at Borisov.

title: Battle of Ligny
description: Fought on 16 June 1815French troops of the Armée du Nord under the command of Napoleon I defeated part of a Prussian army under Field Marshal Blücher.

title: Battle of Quatre Bras
description: Fought on 16 June 1815, as a preliminary engagement to the decisive Battle of Waterloo that occurred two days later.

title: Battle of Pultusk
description: Took place on December 26th 1806 Despite their strong numerical superiority and artillery, the Russians under the lead of General of Cavalry Levin von Benningsen underwent heavy French attacks by Marshal Jean Lannes.

title: Battle of Golymin
description: Fought on the same day as Pultusk, December 26th, this was another intense winter engagement between French and Russian forces in Poland.

title: Battle of Guttstadt-Deppen
description: A violent engagement in the Polish/Prussian region during the prelude to the final campaign of the Fourth Coalition.

title: Battle of Heilsberg
description: Took place on 10 June 1807. Napoleon confronted Russian general Bennigsen and his army. The battle was a bloody engagement that lasted for several hours, with heavy casualties on both sides.

title: Siege of Danzig
description: The Siege of Danzig (now Gdańsk, Poland) by French forces under Marshal Lefebvre, acting as the corps commander of the Grande Armée, during the War of the Fourth Coalition.

title: Battle of Ostrolenka
description: Fought on 16 February 1807 between a French force under General of Division Anne Jean Marie René Savary and a Russian force under Lieutenant General Ivan Essen.

title: Battle of Znaim
description: Following defeat at the Battle of Wagram, Archduke Charles retreated north into Bohemia hoping to regroup his battered forces. The French army had also suffered in the battle and did not give immediate pursuit. But two days after the battle, Napoleon ordered his troops north intending to defeat the Austrians once and for all.

title: Walcheren Campaign
description: The Walcheren Campaign was an unsuccessful British expedition to the Kingdom of Holland in 1809 intended to open another front in the Austrian Empire's struggle with France during the War of the Fifth Coalition.

title: Attack of Flushing
description: The British had captured Flushing on the day of his arrival to the war zone after a ferocious bombardment, and the surrounding towns on August 15th.

title: Battle of Ebelsberg
description: A bloody French assault to take a key bridge over the Traun River, clearing the way to Vienna.

title: Battle of Paris
description: Allied forces  including Prussian, Russian, and Austrian troops, marched into Paris, effectively ending French domination in Europe.

title: Battle of the Côa
description: Took place on 24 July 1810, on the banks of the Côa River, in the borderlands between Portugal and Spain. It was fought between a French division under Marshal Auguste de Marmont and a British division under General Robert Craufurd.

title: Landing at Vedbæk
description: British troops landed at Vedbæk on August 16th 1807 and began besieging Copenhagen.

title: Battle of Køge
description: Fought on 29 August 1807 between British troops besieging Copenhagen and Danish militia raised on Sjælland.

title: Battle of Classens Have
description: Fought on August 31st 1807 between British troops and Danish volunteers.

title: Embargo against Franceis declared.
description: England declares an embargo against France and its allies, including Denmark-Norway.

title: Napoleon's first abdication
description: Napoleon abdicates the French throne in favor of his son, who is not accepted by the Allies occupying Paris.

title: Signing of the Treaty of Fontainebleau
description: Signed between Napoleon Bonaparte and the Allies, ending the War of the Sixth Coalition.

title: Treaty of Saint Petersburg
description: England and Russia signed the Treaty of Saint Petersburg, sealing an alliance against France

title: Departure of the expedition to Egypt.
description: Aboard a fleet of 55 warships and 280 transport vessels, Napoleon sailed to Egypt.

title: Battle of Polotsk
description: It tool place from October 18th to the 20th during Napoleon's invasion of Russia.In this encounter the Russians under General Peter Wittgenstein attacked and defeated a Franco-Bavarian force under Laurent Gouvion Saint-Cyr.

title: Battle of Blanc-Nez and Gris-Nez
description: Fought on 18 July 1805 between a combined flotilla of the Batavian Navy and French Imperial Navy under Carel Hendrik Ver Huell and a Royal Navy fleet under Lord Keith during the War of the Third Coalition.

title: Invasion of Surinam
description: The invasion of Surinam, on April 25th 1804, was a British military campaign which resulted in the capture and occupation of the Dutch colony of Surinam in 1804.

title: Battle of Diamond Rock
description: Took place between 31 May and 2 June 1805 during the Napoleonic Wars, when a Franco-Spanish force dispatched under Captain Julien Cosmao was able to retake Diamond Rock, on the approach to Fort-de-France, from the British forces that had occupied it over a year before.

title: Battle of Cape Finisterre
description: On July 22nd 1805, the British fleet under Admiral Robert Calder fought an indecisive naval battle against the combined Franco-Spanish fleet which was returning from the West Indies.

title: Battle of Donauwörth
description: This was the first engagement of Napoleon's 1805 Austrian campaign. French forces under marshals Joachim Murat and Nicolas Soult beat an Austrian army corps under Kienmayer on October 7th 1805 at Donauwörth and crossed the Danube.

title: Battle of Wertingen
description: On October 8th 1805, French forces led by Marshals Murat and Jean Lannes attacked a small Austrian corps commanded by Feldmarschall-Leutnant Franz Xaver von Auffenberg.

title: Battle of Günzburg
description: On October 9th 1805, Marshall Ney ordered a French division to seize a crossing over the Danube River at Günzburg in the face of a Habsburg Austrian army led by Feldmarschall-Leutnant Karl Mack von Lieberich.

title: Battle of Haslach-Jungingen
description: Also known as the Battle of Albeck, fought on October 11th 1805 at Ulm-Jungingen north of Ulm at the Danube between French and Austrian forces, was part of the Ulm campaign

title: Battle of Memmingen
description: It occurred on 14 October that year and culminated in the surrender of Karl Spangen to Jean-de-Dieu Soult's 4th Army Corps.

title: Battle of Elchingen
description: Fought on 14th October 1805, French forces under Michel Ney routed an Austrian corps led by Johann Sigismund Riesch.

title: Battle of Verona
description: Fought on October 18th 1805 between the French Army of Italy under the command of André Masséna and an Austrian army led by Archduke Charles.

title: Battle of Caldiero
description: Took place on October 30th 1805, pitting the French Armée d'Italie under Marshal André Masséna against an Austrian army under the command of Archduke Charles.

title: Battle of Mehrnbach
description: Took place on October 30th 1805, shortly after the surrender of the Austrian troops in Ulm, from where the road to Vienna lay open to the Grande Armée.

title: Battle of Wischau
description: Occurred on November 28th 1805 between the Russian and French armies. The conflict resulted in a minor Russian success.

title: Battle of Saltanovka
description: Also known as the Battle of Mohilew, took place on 23 July 1812 during the early stages of the 1812 French invasion of Russia.

title: Battle of Klyastitsy
description: Also called the Battle of Yakubovo or the Battle of Oboyarshchina, was a series of military engagements that took place on 30 July [O.S. 18 July] 1812 – 1 August 1812.

title: Battle of Aboukir
description: Fought on July 25th 1799 between the French Army of the Orient under Napoleon Bonaparte and an Ottoman army under Mustafa Pasha.

title: Battle of Abensberg
description: Took place on April 20th 1809 between a Franco-German force under the command of Emperor Napoleon I of France and a reinforced Austrian corps led by Feldmarschall-Leutnant Archduke Louis of Austria.

title: Battle of Landshut
description: Took place on April 21st 1809 between the French, Württembergers (VIII Corps) and Bavarians (VII Corps) under Napoleon, and the Austrians under the General Johann von Hiller.

title: Battle of Neumarkt-Sankt Veit
description: On April 24th 1809 saw a Franco-Bavarian force led by Marshal Jean-Baptiste Bessières face an Austrian Empire army commanded by Johann von Hiller.

title: Battle of Radzymin
description: Took place on 25 April 1809, during the brief Polish–Austrian War. The battle occurred at Radzymin, some 20 kilometres (12 mi) north-east of Warsaw.

title: Battle of Gospic
description: On May 21st 1809, Marmont located Rebrovich's forces deployed behind the Lika River near Gospić.

title: Battle of La Piave
description: Fought on May 8th 1809 between the Franco-Italian army under the command of Eugène de Beauharnais and an Austrian army led by Archduke John of Austria.

title: Battle of Wörgl
description: Fought on May 13th 1809, when a Bavarian force under French Marshal François Joseph Lefebvre attacked an Austrian Empire detachment commanded by Johann Gabriel Chasteler de Courcelles.

title: Battle of Tarvis
description: The two day battle took place from May 16th to May 17th 1809.

title: Battle of Malborghetto
description: The fortress was stormed by French troops on May 15th to May 17th 1809.

title: Battle of Linz-Urfahr
description: The Battle of Linz-Urfahr was fought on May 17th 1809, where the Austrian Empire Army fought against troops from two of Emperor Napoleon's allies, the Kingdom of Württemberg and the Kingdom of Saxony.

title: Battle of Sankt Michael
description: On May 25th 1809, Paul Grenier's French corps crushed Franz Jellacic's Austrian division at Sankt Michael in Obersteiermark, Austria.

title: Battle of the Basque Roads
description: The battle lasted from April 11th to the 24th 1809 and was a major naval battle of the Napoleonic Wars, fought in the narrow Basque Roads at the mouth of the Charente River on the Biscay coast of France.

title: Battle of Raab
description: It was fought on June 14th 1809 between Franco-Italian forces and Habsburg forces. Most of the Habsburg army consisted of poorly trained Landwehr and militia.

title: Battle of Graz
description: It was fought on June 14th 1809 between Franco-Italian forces and Habsburg forces. Most of the Habsburg army consisted of poorly trained Landwehr and militia.

title: Combat of Korneuburg
description: It was a relatively minor rearguard action fought by Austrian VI Korps of the Kaiserlich-königliche Hauptarmee under Johann von Klenau against elements of the French IV Corps of the Armée d'Allemagne, under the command of Claude Legrand.

title: Armistice of Cherasco
description: The Armistice of Cherasco was a treaty signed on April 26th 1796 between Napoleon Bonaparte and the Savoy kingdom.

title: Battle of Gefrees
description: Fought on July 8th 1809 during the War of the Fifth Coalition between a joint force of Austrians and Brunswickers under the command of General Kienmayer and a French force under the command of General Junot.

title: Battle of Hollabrunn
description: Was a rearguard action fought on July 9th 1809 by Austrian VI Korps of the Kaiserlich-königliche Hauptarmee under Johann von Klenau against elements of the French IV Corps of the Armée d'Allemagne, under the command of André Masséna

title: Battle of Möckern
description: Was a series of heavy clashes on April 5th 1813 between allied Prusso-Russian troops under Russian commander Peter Wittgenstein and Napoleonic French forces under Eugène de Beauharnais.

title: Battle of Luneburg
description: On April 2nd 1813, Allied Russians and Prussians were victorious over a French and Saxon division.

title: Battle of Luckau
description: Prussian and Russian forces under General Friedrich Wilhelm von Bülow defeated part of a French-Allied corps under Marshal Nicolas Oudinot on June 4th 1813.

title: Battle of Lützen
description: Prussian and Russian forces under General Friedrich Wilhelm von Bülow defeated part of a French-Allied corps under Marshal Nicolas Oudinot on June 4th 1813.

title: Battle of Haynau
description: Fought on May 26th 1813, between Prussian cavalry under the command of General Gebhard Leberecht von Blücher and a French infantry division under the command of General Nicolas Joseph Maison

title: Battle of Grobbeeren
description: Occurred on August 23rd 1813 in neighboring Blankenfelde and Sputendorf between the Prussian III Corps under Friedrich von Bülow and the Franco-Saxon VII Corps under Jean Reynier.

title: Battle of the Katzbach
description: Fought on August 26th 1813, was a major battle of the Napoleonic Wars between the forces of the First French Empire under Marshal MacDonald and a Russo-Prussian army of the Sixth Coalition under Prussian Marshal Graf von Blücher.

title: Battle of Dresden
description: Fought on August 26th - 27th 1813, was a major engagement of the Napoleonic Wars. The battle took place around the city of Dresden in modern-day Germany, then the capital of the Kingdom of Saxony.

title: Battle of Hagelberg
description: Fought on August 27th 1813, was a major engagement of the Napoleonic Wars. The battle took place around the village of Hagelberg in modern-day Germany, then the capital of the Kingdom of Saxony.

title: Battle of Kulm
description: Fought on August 29th - 30th 1813, was a major engagement of the Napoleonic Wars. The battle took place around the village of Kulm in modern-day Czech Republic, then the capital of the Kingdom of Bohemia.

title: Battle of Dennewitz
description: Fought on September 6th 1813, was a major engagement of the Napoleonic Wars. The battle took place around the village of Dennewitz in modern-day Germany, then the capital of the Kingdom of Saxony.

title: Battle of Wartenburg
description: Took place on October 3rd 1813 between the French IV Corps commanded by General Henri Gatien Bertrand and the Allied Army of Silesia, principally the I Corps of General Ludwig von Yorck with the personal participation of the army commander, Gebhard L. von Blücher.

title: Appointment of the Commander of the Army of Portugal
description: Marshal André Masséna was appointed to command the Army of Portugal on April 17, 1810.

title: Battle of Sacile
description: Also known as the Battle of Fontana Fredda, saw an Austrian army commanded by Archduke John of Austria defeat a Franco-Italian army led by Eugène de Beauharnais and force it to retreat on April 16th 1809.

title: Batalla de la Poza de Sta Isabel
description: Also known as the capture of the Rosily squadron, took place on June 14th 1808 in Cádiz following the Dos de Mayo Uprising against French troops in Madrid.
